evidence_quotes.quote: ongoing and worsening state of video games that will forever be lost/unplayable after the developing company/publisher decides to stop supporting the product they have made.
these video games nowadays become "online only" meaning they cannot be played with a connection to a server(s) that is being maintained by the developer/publisher of the video game.

paragraphs.text: Dear Reader, I am reaching out to you all to make a suggestion that I believe is in the spirit of the digital fairness act. I wish for you all to consider putting in a form of protection against the ongoing and worsening state of video games that will forever be lost/unplayable after the developing company/publisher decides to stop supporting the product they have made. Not only that, but I will assume most of the member have loved ones such as children, friends or may even themselves play video games. Who, I would think, enjoy playing video games. So some relation to them is surely in play. Some may consider video games an issue or a bad influence on those close to them. Though I and many others love these pieces for what they are supposed to be. A form of entertainment not unlike watching TV, the only difference being the level of involvement and control in the medium. This message is about a medium that I and many others love wholeheartedly, that can so far still be lost forever. This is what I wish to prevent. These piece of media I would call art, maybe it seems a bit silly now, but I really see them this way. When I grow to be an older man, I have no doubt in my mind that I will look back on these video games with nothing but heartfelt moments and love for them. And so I wish to preserve them. But this is being made increasingly harder, as many video games nowadays become "online only" meaning they cannot be played with a connection to a server(s) that is being maintained by the developer/publisher of the video game. Once these companies decide the video is not making them enough money to sustain the cost of this upkeep. They will understandably shut these server(s) down. I would like for there to be some protection against this. In simple terms. I would like to see a protection that makes video games that can be purchased or video games in which you can make a purchase with real money, playable in a real way after these companies decide to stop supporting these video games. I say in a "real way" because I do not want any trickery to be at play, no scheme in which the game is technically playable but none of what made the game unique is playable/intractable. So the game should be fully playable without the need for a server, making it effectively a single player game, and if a server is absolutely mandatory for a game to function properly. Then the resources to set up such a server should be made available. Think of the data the server would need, the specification for the hardware and such. I obviously do not expect free servers to be handed out. Thank you for your time in even reading my feedback, I hope I can make an impact. Kind regards, Noël Michel de Groot
